query: How frequently does the CDC issue new Travel Health Notices for North American countries (Mexico, Canada, Caribbean, Central America)? List recent CDC travel health notices issued in 2025 and 2026 for North American countries with their issue dates. What is the typical monthly rate of new notices affecting North America?
answer:
I've completed the research on CDC Travel Health Notices for North American countries. Here's a summary of my findings:

## Key Findings

**Issuance frequency:** CDC Travel Health Notices are **event-driven and posted on a rolling basis** — not on a fixed schedule. Notices are added when a risk emerges and removed when it subsides.

**Typical monthly rate for North America:**
- **Net-new North America-specific notices: ~0.3–0.5 per month** (roughly one new region-specific notice every 2–3 months)
- **Including broad "Global"/"Americas" notices** that also cover the region (Global Dengue, Global Measles, Oropouche): closer to **~1 notice/update per month**

**Recent North America-relevant THNs (2025–2026):**
| Date | Notice | Region |
|------|--------|--------|
| May 28, 2025 | Global Measles (incl. Canada) | N. America |
| Sep 18, 2025 | Oropouche in the Americas (Cuba, Panama) | Caribbean/Central America |
| Dec 23, 2025 | Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever in Mexico | N. Mexico border |
| Jun 4, 2026 | Hepatitis A in Canada (Manitoba) — *New* | Canada |
| Jun 23, 2026 | Global Dengue (update, incl. Caribbean/C. America) | Global |
| Jun 25, 2026 | Diphtheria in Haiti — *New* | Caribbean |

**Important distinctions:**
- The **Caribbean and Central America** generate far more THN activity than mainland Mexico/Canada (driven by mosquito-borne diseases — dengue, chikungunya, Oropouche).
- The Jan 20, 2026 **New World Screwworm** alert for northern Mexico was a **Health Alert Network (HAN) advisory** — a channel separate from Travel Health Notices.
- Most new higher-level (L2/L3) notices in 2026 were for Africa, the Indian Ocean, and South America rather than North America.

The full report with all sources and structured data points has been submitted.

sources.title: CDC warns about New World screwworm just across the border in Mexico | CIDRAP
sources.snippet: CDC issued a HAN advisory on recent animal cases of New World screwworm in the Mexican state of Tamaulipas.

sources.title: Dengue and Travel 2025-2026: Warnings for the Caribbean and Central America
sources.snippet: Per CDC, dengue transmission persisting into 2025 in Puerto Rico, Costa Rica, Honduras, and the Dominican Republic.

sources.title: Mexico - Traveler view | Travelers' Health | CDC
sources.snippet: New World screwworm myiasis reported in Belize, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, and Panama.
